products.name: Surgicover
products.description: A specialised peri-operative enteral supplement formulated to optimise surgical recovery, preserve lean muscle mass, and accelerate wound healing across all phases of surgery. It serves as a metabolic bridge throughout the surgical timeline — from pre-operative prehabilitation through home recovery.
products.target_audience: Hospitals, surgical centres, clinical dietitians, surgeons, B2B procurement teams, and post-surgical patients

variants.name: Vanilla
variants.description: Smooth, creamy flavour ideal for general patient compliance, especially for patients experiencing post-operative taste alterations

variants.name: Chocolate
variants.description: Rich flavour for patients with reduced appetite or post-operative taste changes (dysgeusia)

variants.name: Diabetic Cover (Dry Fruits)
variants.description: Available in Dry Fruits flavour only. Zero added sucrose, sweetened with sucralose — specifically safe for Type 1 and Type 2 diabetic and pre-diabetic surgical patients requiring strict glycaemic control. The mild, natural dry fruits taste also suits patients experiencing post-operative anorexia, nausea, or taste alterations.

products.preparation: Measure 150ml of lukewarm water or milk into a clean container. Add exactly one heaped scoop (20g) of Surgicover powder. Stir or shake briskly until completely dissolved. Consume immediately after preparation. Recommended 1-2 servings daily or as directed by the attending clinical specialist.
products.preparation_warnings: Do NOT mix with hot or boiling liquids — temperatures above 40C / 104F denature proteins and degrade heat-sensitive vitamins. Use only lukewarm or room-temperature liquids. Do NOT consume within 2 hours before anaesthesia (nil-by-mouth NPO window).
products.ingredients: Soya Protein Isolate, Defatted Soya Flour, Skimmed Milk Powder, Sucralose (INS955), Di-basic Calcium Phosphate, Cyanocobalamin (B12), Thiamine Mononitrate (B1), Riboflavin (B2), Niacinamide (B3), Calcium D-Pantothenate (B5), Pyridoxine Hydrochloride (B6), Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D), Folic Acid (B9), Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C), Ferric Ammonium Citrate (Iron), Manganese Sulphate, Copper Sulphate, L-Arginine, L-Leucine, Preservative INS211, Permitted Flavours.
products.regulatory_label: CONTAINS NATURALLY OCCURRING SUGARS. Contains Milk and Soy allergens. Not for parenteral or IV use. Zero Added Sugar (Sucrose) claim is lawful under FSSAI Non-Addition of Sugars guidelines — naturally occurring lactose from Skimmed Milk Powder is present but <0.1g per serving.

nutrition_per_20g_serving.B_vitamins: B1 37% RDA, B2 37% RDA, B3 44% RDA, B5 13% RDA, B6 13% RDA, B9 27% RDA, B12 27% RDA

clinical_use.pre_op_prehabilitation_7_to_5_days: Start 5-7 days before elective surgery: 1 serving twice daily (BID). Builds protein reserves, optimises micronutrient levels, strengthens immunity. Especially beneficial for BMI <18.5, >10% weight loss in 6 months, or major GI/orthopaedic/oncological procedures.
clinical_use.pre_op_window_24_to_2_hrs: Solid foods stopped 6hrs pre-surgery. Within 2hrs of surgery: strict nil-by-mouth (NPO) — Surgicover MUST NOT be consumed in this window.
clinical_use.post_op_day_1_clear_liquids: Post-Op Day 1: Clear liquids only (water, broth). Surgicover not yet introduced.
clinical_use.post_op_days_2_to_5_soft_diet: Post-Op Days 2-5: Introduce Surgicover at 1 serving (20g in 150ml lukewarm water) twice daily as patient transitions to soft/pureed diet.
clinical_use.post_op_days_6_to_14_and_beyond: Post-Op Days 6-14+: Continue 1-2 servings daily. Can be blended into pureed oatmeal or warm porridges.
clinical_use.home_recovery_14_to_30_days: Maintain 1-2 servings daily for 14-30 days post-discharge. Administer 20-30 minutes post-physiotherapy to optimise muscle protein synthesis.

key_clinical_points: Zero added sucrose — prevents post-operative hyperglycaemia which impairs wound healing and increases infection risk
L-Arginine (200mg per serving) — boosts nitric oxide for wound perfusion, collagen deposition, and immune T-cell response
L-Leucine (100mg per serving) — activates mTORC1 to stimulate muscle protein synthesis and prevent muscle wasting
PDCAAS 1.0 protein — clinically equivalent to whey in amino acid quality with superior GI tolerance for post-op patients
Lactose <0.1g per serving — safe for patients with secondary lactose intolerance
Aligned with ERAS (Enhanced Recovery After Surgery) protocols across all major surgical departments

knowledge_sections.title: Department Applications
knowledge_sections.content: Orthopaedics (ACL, THA, TKA): muscle preservation, bone mineralisation. Urology/Prostatectomy: cavernous microvascular perfusion via L-Arginine/NO pathway. General Surgery (laparotomy, hernia): gut barrier support, fascial collagen synthesis. Gynaecology/Hysterectomy: pelvic floor healing, no constipation risk (SPI does not delay gastric emptying). Oncology: counters cancer cachexia, L-Leucine offsets tumour-induced mTORC1 suppression. GI Surgery (colectomy, pancreatectomy): prevents gut hyperpermeability and hypoalbuminaemia. Neurosurgery/TBI: cerebral microperfusion, B-complex vitamins for axonal repair. Vascular Surgery: ischaemia-reperfusion protection, improves graft and AV fistula patency. Cardiothoracic/Sternotomy: Vitamin D for sternal osteogenesis, zero fat/sucrose compatible with cardiac diet. Plastics/Free Flap: NO-mediated flap perfusion, collagen cross-linking. ENT/Tonsillectomy: smooth non-acidic liquid for odynophagia, syringe-administrable. Dental/Jaw Wiring: liquid nutrition through wired teeth, prevents jaw muscle wasting, Vitamin D/Calcium for bone callus.

knowledge_sections.title: Dosing Protocols
knowledge_sections.content: Standard serving: 1 heaped scoop (20g) in 150-200ml lukewarm water or milk. Pre-op prehabilitation (5-7 days before surgery): 2 servings/day (BID). Post-op Day 2-5: 2 servings/day. Post-op Day 6+: 1-2 servings/day. Hypercatabolic states (major GI, ortho, oncology): 3-4 servings/day (TID/QID) = 18-24g protein, 600-800mg L-Arginine per day. Post-exercise: take 20-30 min after physiotherapy for optimal muscle protein synthesis. ENT/dental patients: administer via cup, syringe, or feeding tube. Home recovery: continue 1-2 servings/day for 14-30 days post-discharge. Do not exceed 30g protein per single dose (causes gastric distension and amino acid saturation).

knowledge_sections.title: Safety and Contraindications
knowledge_sections.content: SAFE FOR: Diabetic patients (zero sucrose, sucralose GI=0). Secondary lactose intolerance (lactose <0.1g per serving). ENT and oral surgery (smooth, non-acidic liquid). Cardiac/vascular patients (0.19g fat, zero sucrose). CKD CAUTION: Stage 3+ CKD — monitor total protein, phosphorus, and calcium intake under nephrologist guidance. NOT FOR: Parenteral/IV use. Within 2 hours before anaesthesia (NPO window). ALLERGENS: Contains Milk and Soy. Do NOT mix above 40 C / 104 F.

knowledge_sections.title: How Surgicover Compares
knowledge_sections.content: Versus Ensure HP: No free-form L-Arginine or L-Leucine; contains sucrose. Versus PentaSure HP: Uses whey (lactose risk for post-op patients); contains fructose and maltodextrin (glycaemic). Versus Resource HP (Nestle): Whey and maltodextrin (high GI); no surgical amino acid spike. Surgicover is the only supplement in this segment with a dual free-form L-Arginine (200mg) + L-Leucine (100mg) spike per serving, zero sucrose (sucralose only), and a lactose-compatible SPI protein matrix — purpose-built for the peri-operative window.

knowledge_sections.title: Frequently Asked Questions
knowledge_sections.content: Q: Safe for diabetics? A: Yes — zero sucrose, sucralose GI=0. Diabetic Cover variant is specifically for diabetic/pre-diabetic surgical patients. Q: Lactose concern from Skimmed Milk Powder? A: Lactose per serving <0.1g — far below the 12g clinical threshold. Safe for post-op patients with secondary lactose intolerance. Q: Is 6g protein per serving enough? A: Yes — designed for fractionated dosing (3-4 servings/day = 18-24g protein) to avoid gastric distension and amino acid saturation. Q: Can it be mixed with hot water or tea? A: No — use lukewarm/room-temperature liquids only (below 40 C / 104 F). Q: When to start? A: 5-7 days before surgery; resume from post-op Day 2; continue 14-30 days post-discharge. Q: CKD patients? A: Usable but requires nephrologist/dietitian supervision to manage protein, phosphorus, and calcium. Q: Tube feeding? A: Can be given via syringe or feeding tube for ENT/dental patients — not a complete tube-feed formula. Q: Does it cause constipation? A: No — SPI does not delay gastric emptying; suitable for gynaecological and abdominal surgery patients. Q: Why soya instead of whey? A: SPI is naturally lactose-free and PDCAAS 1.0 equivalent to whey. Post-op patients risk secondary lactose intolerance from whey. SPI also has natural anti-inflammatory properties.

To place an order, use the order form at /order or email orders@sparrowpharmaceuticals.in. Handles B2B procurement for hospitals, surgical centres, and clinical dietitians.
